key_departures.impact: Released with post-June 1 designation on March 11. Murray played only 5 games in 2025 due to injury, with Jacoby Brissett taking over and going 1-11 in 12 starts. Murray signed a one-year veteran minimum deal with the Vikings. The Cardinals carry significant dead money from his 2022 extension. Arizona had already essentially moved on to Brissett mid-season, so the departure formalizes what was already reality — but the franchise is left with no long-term QB answer.

Signed a 3-year, $33M deal ($22M guaranteed) with Dallas. Thompson was a full-time starter alongside Budda Baker for 5 seasons, recording 95 tackles and 1 INT in 15 games in 2025. His departure removes Arizona's best secondary player not named Budda Baker and creates a genuine hole at S2.

Released March 6, saving approximately $9.4M in cap space with $6.8M dead cap. Was a starter on Arizona's interior defensive line. Departure creates depth hole at IDL and motivated the Roy Lopez re-signing.
Released March 5 along with Akeem Davis-Gaither, saving over $10M combined. Nichols was a rotational IDL piece. Loss compounds the interior D-line thinning.
Released March 5. Was a rotational linebacker. Loss contributes to a thin LB corps but was not a featured starter.

Released March 13, freeing $4.9M cap space with a $1.5M dead cap hit. Brown had started 17 games at LG in 2024 and 11 games in 2025 before injury. His departure opened the LG spot now filled by Seumalo. Net neutral — the Cardinals already had Seumalo lined up to replace him.
Allowed to walk into free agency. Was the Cardinals' starting RT. Departure leaves RT as an open competition heading into 2026, addressed partially by Elijah Wilkinson signing.
Hit free agency as a UFA. Had been the starting RG. Departure further opens up the interior OL, which Arizona is addressing through Seumalo and depth signings.
Cardinals declined to tender Demercado, making him a UFA. He signed a 1-year deal with the Chiefs. Depth loss at RB but offset by multiple new signings at the position.

signings.analysis: Best available interior OL on the market. 3yr/$31.5M with $19M guaranteed is a legitimate multi-year investment — this is their guy at LG. Seumalo posted a 73.3 PFF grade in 2025, the 16th-best mark among eligible OL, with the lowest pressure rate of any guard in the league. He slots directly into the starting LG spot vacated by Evan Brown. The 3-year structure with $19M guaranteed signals real commitment. IOL need moves from critical to depth-only. Team still needs RG clarity but Seumalo resolves the premier interior need.

2yr/$12.25M with $8M fully guaranteed — legitimate investment at RB. Allgeier has never missed a game in 4 NFL seasons, rushed for 514 yards and 8 TDs in 2025, and projects as a co-starter alongside James Conner (restructured). Combined with Conner's restructure, Trey Benson, and Bam Knight re-sign, Arizona has a very deep backfield. RB was a top need after Conner's injury-shortened 2025; it is now fully addressed. The team should not spend meaningful draft capital on RB.

2yr/$10M ($7.5M total guarantees, $6.5M at signing) — below the starter-tier AAV for WR. Bourne caught 37 passes for 551 yards in 2025 for SF and has familiarity with new HC Mike LaFleur from their time together in San Francisco. He fills the slot/WR3 role and replaces departed Zay Jones and Greg Dortch. However, at $5M AAV he is a depth-tier signing by market standards, not a true WR2. With Marvin Harrison Jr. locked in as WR1, Arizona still lacks a proven WR2. Need drops one tier but is not fully resolved — WR remains a legitimate draft target, especially in rounds 2-3.

2yr/$11.5M with $7M guaranteed is a meaningful commitment — not a prove-it deal — for an IDL player Arizona knows well (he played for them in 2023-24 under DC Nick Rallis). Lopez posted 2 sacks, 5 QB hits, and 4 TFLs for Detroit in 2025. However, at $5.75M AAV, this is a mid-tier IDL signing. Combined with LJ Collier re-sign (1yr/$2.5M), Arizona has replaced the Tomlinson/Nichols losses with familiar depth, but has not upgraded the ceiling of the IDL. After cutting Tomlinson and Nichols, IDL was a clear need; Lopez + Collier partially address it but don't bring a dominant run-stopper. Still a draft target.

1yr/$5.75M (up to $8M with incentives) — classic bridge/depth signing. Minshew is coming off a knee injury and threw only 13 passes in 2025. He slots as QB2 behind Jacoby Brissett. The Cardinals missed on Malik Willis (signed with Miami, 3yr/$67.5M) and talks with Garoppolo stalled. The 1-year structure with minimal guarantees explicitly signals Arizona is NOT committing to a long-term QB solution in free agency. QB remains a critical need. The Cardinals hold the No. 3 overall pick and are strongly expected to address QB in the draft.

1yr/$3M — a competition/depth signing to replace departed Jalen Thompson. Wingard played 16 games in 2025 with 1 INT, 1 fumble recovery, and 84 tackles. He will compete with Dadrion Taylor-Demerson for the S2 spot alongside Budda Baker. This is not an upgrade over the departed Thompson ($11M/yr at Dallas). A 1-year, $3M deal in a post-Thompson world is a minimally impactful bridge. S remains a meaningful need.

2-year deal — contract terms not yet fully reported, but structure signals a genuine starter-level commitment. Wilkinson started all 17 games at RT for Atlanta in 2025 and was part of an OL that allowed the 4th-fewest sacks in the NFL. He returns to Arizona (was there in 2023) and projects as the starting RT to replace the departed Jonah Williams (OT). The 2-year term is meaningful. However, Wilkinson is 31 and a depth-tier contract likely places him in the $5-7M AAV range. OT need is partially addressed — Wilkinson stabilizes RT in 2026 but the team should still target a developmental tackle in the draft given age and long-term concerns.

1yr/$1.263M — veteran minimum deal. Pryor started 1 game in 2025 in Philadelphia but was a starting RG for Chicago in 2024. Adds depth and competition at RG with Isaiah Adams. With Will Hernandez gone, RG is still technically unresolved, but this is a camp/depth signing not a starter solution. Minimal impact on the IOL need picture.

1-year deal — terms not publicly reported. Williams set career highs in 2025 with 3 sacks, 5 TFLs, and 5 QB hits in 15 games (1 start) for New Orleans. He provides rotational depth along the D-line. A 1-year deal for a player with only 5.5 career sacks in 65 games is a camp/depth move. Does not materially change the IDL need picture beyond adding body count.

1yr/$2.5M — primarily a special teams/returner addition. Duvernay averaged 11 yds/punt return and 26.7 yds/kick return in 2025, the latter ranking 2nd in his career. His receiving production has fallen sharply (17 catches over his last 3 seasons after 90 in his first 3). This is a special teams upgrade, not a WR depth solve. Does not move the needle on the WR need.

1-year re-sign — terms not publicly reported. Knight was a surprise addition to the 53-man roster in 2025, made 8 starts due to Conner/Benson injuries, and totaled 269 yards and 4 TDs on 82 carries. Re-signing adds familiar depth to an already crowded backfield (Conner, Allgeier, Benson, Knight). No material impact on need picture.

1yr/$2.5M — his 4th 1-year deal with Arizona. Collier flashes when healthy (3.5 sacks, 4 TFLs in 2024) but has been injured in 2 of 3 seasons (1 game in 2023, 4 games in 2025). Provides IDL depth and scheme familiarity but given his health history, this is low-confidence depth. Does not move the IDL needle materially.
1-year deal — terms not publicly reported. Replaces longtime Cardinals LS Aaron Brewer. Special teams-only move. No impact on positional needs.
1-year re-sign. Gillikin averaged 51.7 yds/punt in 2025 (career high) in 5 games played before injury. Punter retention, no impact on skill position needs.
1-year re-sign. Ryland hit 75.8% of FGs in 2025 (down from 87.5% in 2024), struggling from distance. Cardinals retained him ahead of free agency. Kicker retention, no impact on skill position needs.
1-year re-sign. Fehoko missed most of 2025 with a broken arm, playing only 8 games. Provides veteran WR depth and special teams presence. No impact on WR need.

Restructured contract keeping Conner in Arizona for 2026 at a reduced $3M cap figure. Was a cut candidate before restructure. Conner was limited to 3 games in 2025 due to injury but remains the nominal RB1 when healthy. Combined with Allgeier signing, the RB room is now well-stocked. Conner's restructure + Allgeier addition fully closes RB as a need.

Restructured contract to retain Murphy-Bunting, who was expected to be a cap casualty. His retention keeps a starting-caliber CB on the roster but the restructure to a 1-year deal signals Arizona is not committing to him long-term. SMB was not a dominant CB in 2025. His retention partially stabilizes CB depth alongside Garrett Williams and Marco Wilson but does not elevate the position.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Kyler Murray released, Malik Willis went to Miami, Garoppolo talks stalled. Minshew signing (1yr/$5.75M) is explicit bridge depth — he will back up Brissett. The Cardinals hold the No. 3 overall pick. QB remains a top-1 need for this franchise and the draft is the expected avenue. Do not downgrade this need.

Seumalo signing (3yr/$31.5M, $19M gtd) is the best available guard on the market slotted directly into the starting LG role. This is a genuine multi-year commitment and fully resolves the LG problem. RG is a competition (Adams vs. Pryor) but that is a depth matter now, not a critical need. IOL drops to depth/luxury tier.

Conner restructured (1yr/$3M), Allgeier signed (2yr/$12.25M, $8M gtd), Knight re-signed, Benson still on roster. Arizona now has legitimate 3-headed depth at RB. Allgeier's $8M in full guarantees on a 2-year deal is a real investment. RB is done — do not draft here unless it falls in Day 3.

Jonah Williams (OT starter) departed to FA; Wilkinson (2yr deal) returns to fill RT. Paris Johnson Jr. anchors LT. Wilkinson is 31 and a bridge solution at best. The team needs a developmental RT for the future. OT drops one tier but should still be addressed in rounds 2-4 of the draft.

Bourne signing (2yr/$10M, $7.5M gtd) fills the WR3/slot role vacated by Zay Jones and Greg Dortch. However, Bourne at $5M AAV is a depth-tier signing at WR. Marvin Harrison Jr. is the WR1, but Arizona still lacks a proven WR2. With a new system under Mike LaFleur, another quality weapon is needed. Keep at tier 2 — WR2 is a legitimate rounds 2-3 target.

Lost Tomlinson (released) and Nichols (released). Re-signed Roy Lopez (2yr/$11.5M, $7M gtd) and L.J. Collier (1yr/$2.5M), plus added Jonah Williams (DL, 1yr). Lopez brings familiarity with Rallis's scheme and provides a legitimate rotational presence. The IDL room is deeper than it was, but lacks a dominant run-stuffer. Drops one tier — addressable in rounds 3-5 of the draft.

Jalen Thompson departed to Dallas on a 3yr/$33M deal — a starting safety at prime age. Wingard (1yr/$3M) is competition with Taylor-Demerson for the S2 spot, not an upgrade. Budda Baker remains elite at S1, but S2 is now a genuine hole. S escalates from tier 3 to tier 2 — a legitimate rounds 2-4 draft target.

Murphy-Bunting restructured/retained, Garrett Williams developing as a young CB. No notable CB departed or signed. SMB's 1-year restructure is a bridge. CB need is unchanged — still a depth/day-3 concern, not a top priority.

No EDGE signings or departures of note in free agency. Arizona's pass rush remains deficient. Multiple analysts project Arizona taking an EDGE at No. 3 overall (e.g., Miami's Rueben Bain Jr.). EDGE remains a critical need entering the draft.
Akeem Davis-Gaither released; no LB signed in free agency. Arizona's LB corps remains thin. Need is unchanged. Should target a starter-caliber LB in rounds 2-4.

key_additions.headline: Pro Bowl guard and best available IOL on market; anchors overhauled starting left guard spot
Durable former 1,000-yard rusher provides insurance for injury-prone James Conner
Slot receiver with prior LaFleur connection fills WR3 hole left by Jones and Dortch departures
Arizona fan favorite returns; familiar with DC Rallis's scheme after two previous stints with team
Starting RT who played all 17 games in Atlanta steps in to replace departed Jonah Williams
Bridge backup behind Brissett after Cardinals miss on Malik Willis; QB need still unresolved

key_losses.headline: Franchise QB released after 7 seasons and 87 starts; Cardinals carry massive dead money from 2022 extension
Starting S2 alongside Budda Baker for 5 seasons departs, creating genuine hole in Arizona's secondary
Cap casualty release thins interior D-line depth heading into critical rebuild offseason
Starting right tackle allowed to walk, leaving OT as an unresolved position entering the draft
Starting RG departs, though Seumalo signing and Isaiah Adams competition partially offset the loss
Starting LG cap casualty cleared path for Isaac Seumalo; net-neutral roster impact
